model_history.notes: Backward elimination + retention curve signals. Dropped z_score, vz_score, novelty
Milestone: CV > 0.50. Channel momentum + concept×momentum interactions
Rebased to n=203 (different holdout). Pacing + hook signals. CV breaks 0.50
Forward selection. Trajectory signals (view_accel_log, w2_w1_ratio) add +0.16. LOO=0.678
n=210. deriv_std replaces deriv_entropy+smoothed_slope. OLS optimal vs GBM/RF/etc.
CORRUPTED: Contains circular outcome features (like_ratio, svf_log, engage_ratio, rpv). R²=0.94 is meaningless for prediction.

kept_signals.meaning: Non-linear keep rate — accelerating returns above 75%. Marginal keep above 75 doubles hit rate.
Biggest single-point retention drop. Signals initial virality spike (high initial replay → big drop). Counter-intuitive: bigger drops = more views.
Midpoint retention squared. Non-linear mid-video engagement. Videos maintaining middle engagement outperform.
Subscriber vs non-subscriber retention gap. Smaller gap = broader appeal = more views.
Slope of 5-pt moving avg retention curve. Captures overall decay trend independently of max_cliff.
Pacing complexity — entropy of retention curve derivative. Varied pacing beats monotonic.
Duration × retention = total watch time proxy. Algorithm rewards absolute watch minutes.
Word count of AI-generated videoIdea. Longer/richer video concepts predict slightly more views.
Title contains making/build/creat/construct. 34 videos avg 19.7M vs 5.6M rest.
Indestructible concept × previous keep rate. RELEASE STRATEGY: indestructible after high-keep = super-viral.
Previous video's log10 views. Channel momentum — algorithm gives bigger push after a hit.
Previous video's keep rate. Audience priming effect. Weight negative = rebound after low keep.
Log10 of day3-7 avg / day1 views. Early trajectory signal. Strongest predictor of total views.
Week2 / Week1 view ratio. Sustained growth predicts total views. Second-wave videos outperform.

top_discoveries.meaning: STRONGEST signal. First 7-day view sum predicts total views even after controlling keep. Early algorithmic push is the mechanism.
StdDev of retention curve derivative = dramatic pacing. Varied rate-of-change beats monotonic decay.
deriv_std / retentionVariation = dramatic pacing efficiency. Sharp turns at specific moments + smooth overall arc.
Viral videos DON'T peak day 1. Lower day-1 share = more total views. Algorithmic sustained push.
Day7/Day1 view ratio predicts total views. Sustained momentum is the key to virality.
Retention at 75%/25% predicts views. The narrative middle separates viral from average.
Counter-intuitive: BIGGER max retention drops = MORE views. Dramatic narrative turns drive virality.
Lower like/view ratio = MORE views. Viral videos reach beyond core audience, diluting engagement per view.
body_hold*10 + d7d1 - retVar*5. Best multi-signal combo — captures 3 independent dimensions.
46-60s = sweet spot (28/98 hit 10M+, 2.9M median). 15-30s = death zone (0/11, 513K median).
'Making' = $24M avg (23 videos). Build/creation content is strongest format.
keep>=75 AND maxCliff>=0.15: 45% of videos, 36% hit 10M+. Practical pre-screening formula.
Non-linear: <75% keep = 3-12% hit 10M+. 75-80% = 25%. 80-85% = 41%. Marginal keep above 75 doubles hit rate.

causal_findings.meaning: Forward-momentum words (so/but/wait/actually) show r=0.05 vs retention but r=0.23 vs keep. Connector/pivot words predict KEEP not retention.
Pivot words (but/however/wait/suddenly) per 100 words. r=0.26 vs keep, r=0.10 vs retention. Narrative surprise drives initial hook.
Hook concept callback in last 20%. Only r=0.05 vs end retention (weak). 46% callback rate. Callbacks help keep, not retention.
Fraction of seconds with audio+visual+concept all active. Weak signal (r=0.07). Multi-channel completeness is noise at n=203.
Total above-baseline retention area vs views. r=0.27. Watch time above baseline correlates with views.
Narrative arc quality (hook→climax→payoff). Weak: r=0.13 vs keep, r=-0.01 vs retention. Arc quality doesn't predict retention.
Action word density in hook segment vs keep. r=0.15. Physical/action hooks slightly better than talking hooks.

retention_patterns.evidence: Videos peaking at 60-80% get ~10x more views than early-peaking (5.2M vs 530K median). Building narrative arc beats front-loaded content.

retention_patterns.pattern: Visceral words retain, transition words kill
retention_patterns.evidence: Retention GAIN: visceral/physical (painful +5.9%, difference +4.3%, hurt +2.5%). Retention DROP: transitions (so -11.2%, however -8.6%). Show don't tell.
Videos at 95-99% retention percentile: +8.0% above baseline while rest is -0.4%. Final 5% of video is THE differentiator for mega-virality.
56% of videos have worst retention in first 20%. 10-20% is the critical make-or-break transition zone.
Physical challenge visuals +12% at peaks. Product/food shots without human anchor cause drops.

retention_patterns.pattern: Speech rate: fast = retained, pauses = dropped
retention_patterns.evidence: Speech rate r=+0.24 vs retention. Long pauses >1s: r=-0.22 vs retention. Pacing speed matters.
'would happen' +4.3%, 'my body' +3.3% retain. Transitional setups 'so, i' -10.4% kill retention. Bigrams matter.
'crazy' at Q1=+0.000, Q4=+0.060. Visceral words 60x more effective later in narrative — context amplifies impact.

retention_patterns.pattern: Vocab diversity hurts keep but helps retention
retention_patterns.evidence: Type-token ratio: r=-0.33 vs keep but r=+0.13 vs retention. Simple vocabulary hooks viewers, but diversity sustains.
Duration r=-0.57 vs retention (longer = lower retention) but duration×retention r=+0.35 vs views. Algorithm rewards total watch minutes.

discarded_reasons.hook_clarity: LLM vision score 1-10. r=+0.21 vs views individually but hurts regression (too noisy at n=203). r=-0.02 vs keep.
discarded_reasons.visual_surprise: LLM vision score 1-10. r=+0.30 vs keep but too noisy for regression. Delta=-0.037.
discarded_reasons.face_presence: 80% of videos have faces. r=+0.12 vs views but hurts model. Too uniform a distribution.
discarded_reasons.vz_score: Removed in backward elimination — captured by keep_sq and other features. Originally r=+0.30 individually.
discarded_reasons.z_score: Removed in backward elimination — captured by other features in v6.
discarded_reasons.net_novelty: Perfectly collinear with novelty - cognitive_load already in model.
discarded_reasons.roughness: Mean abs 2nd derivative. r=+0.12. Collinear with max_cliff — doesn't add incremental value.
discarded_reasons.title_caps_ratio: Uppercase ratio in title. Holdout +0.024 but CV drops. Unstable/noise at n=203.
discarded_reasons.hook_word_density: Dropped in backward elimination v16. Captured by other features (has_text_overlay, hook_silence).
discarded_reasons.fft_features: Signal-processing features from retention curve FFT. All hurt — curve info already captured by max_cliff.
discarded_reasons.knn_k7: KNN regression doesn't beat OLS at n=203. Best k=7: holdout 0.538 vs OLS 0.566.
discarded_reasons.curve_clusters: Retention curve clustering (k=2,3,4). No improvement over linear features.
discarded_reasons.LLM_content_scores: 5+ rounds of LLM-scored content attributes (hook quality, specificity, open loop). Consistently too noisy at n=200.
discarded_reasons.duration_buckets: Only 10 shorts in dataset. Long videos R²=0.50 stratified but buckets don't help regression.
discarded_reasons.deep_curve_features: 66 deep retention curve features tested. All collinear with max_cliff. Curve signal fully captured.
discarded_reasons.RF_GBM_at_n203: Random Forest and GBM both underperform OLS at n=203. RF overfits. OLS dominates at small n.
discarded_reasons.upload_day: Saturday avg 14M views but CV delta < 0.01. Day of week is noise.
discarded_reasons.piecewise_keep: Piecewise linear split on keep=75. Holdout +0.06 but CV worse and higher variance.
discarded_reasons.nonsub_fraction: DATA LEAK: r=0.86 vs views. Algorithm pushes to non-subscribers. Consequence not cause.
